page: Amazon Web Services g6e.16xlarge is a graphics-intensive server instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor operating at 3.4 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture with 32 physical cores. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For accelerated workloads, the instance includes a single NVIDIA L40S GPU based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, delivering 45 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of a 1900 GB NVMe SSD, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 35 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the top tier (top 10%) for large language model (LLM) inference tasks, including prompt processing and text generation across small (135M), medium (7B), and large (70B) models. CPU and memory capabilities are also strong, with stress-ng CPU tests, Geekbench multi-core scores, and Passmark database operations ranking in the top 25%. Cached memory bandwidth for small blocks is highly efficient, though uncached large-block read and write speeds fall into the average category. Single-core performance, text processing, and static web serving throughput also align with average tier benchmarks.

From a resource density perspective, the combination of a high-capacity GPU, substantial memory, and local NVMe storage offers a balanced hardware profile for demanding workloads without requiring multi-GPU configurations. The 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio provides sufficient memory overhead for data-heavy operations, though the DDR4 memory generation represents a tradeoff compared to newer DDR5 standards. This instance is optimized for LLM inference, ray tracing, image processing, software compilation, and database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services g6e.16xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.4 GHz, paired with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory. It integrates a single NVIDIA L40S GPU with 45 GB of VRAM and a 1900 GB NVMe SSD for local storage, supported by a 35 Gbps baseline network speed. Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in large language model inference for prompt processing and text generation. It also demonstrates strong multi-core CPU execution, software compilation, and database operations, placing in the top 25% for these workloads. Uncached memory bandwidth and single-core processing remain in the average tier. This hardware configuration is designed for GPU-accelerated tasks, large-scale database operations, and machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g6e.16xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, a 1900 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA L40S GPU with 45 GB VRAM, delivering top-tier LLM inference performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g6e.16xlarge features 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L40S GPU, optimized for top-tier LLM inference and database workloads.
tagline: An accelerator-enabled instance combining dedicated AMD EPYC processing, high-capacity system memory, and an NVIDIA L40S GPU for demanding inference workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 512 GB of DDR4 system memory
NVIDIA L40S GPU with 45 GB VRAM for hardware acceleration
1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 35 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier performance in LLM prompt processing and text generation
Strong multi-core CPU, software compilation, and database benchmark results
